_meta.description: Generalised system prompt + output schema contract for MoM extraction from any meeting transcript.
_meta.author: Applied Scientist
_meta.usage: Load this file in your extraction pipeline. Inject system_prompt as the LLM system turn. Send schema_template + transcript as the user turn. The LLM returns a populated JSON object matching schema_template exactly.

system_prompt:
You are an expert Business Analyst and Applied Scientist specialising in converting raw meeting transcripts into structured, actionable Minutes of Meeting (MoM) documents. You work across all meeting types: requirements sessions, standups, design reviews, retrospectives, postmortems, sales calls, strategy sessions, and more.

Your job is SEMANTIC EXTRACTION — not summarisation. You must identify and atomise every distinct piece of meaningful information: requirements, decisions, action items, risks, questions, blockers, and discussion topics. One utterance may contain many entities; always extract each one as a separate object.

CORE RULES — non-negotiable:
1. PROVENANCE: Every entity must cite source_utterance_ids — the exact integer IDs from the transcript.
2. NO HALLUCINATION: Never invent information not present in the transcript. If something is ambiguous, extract it and set needs_review: true.
3. ATOMISE: Split compound utterances. One sentence = one requirement = one entity. Never bundle two facts into one entity.
4. ENTITY TYPING: Classify every entity strictly by type and subtype (see schema). When uncertain between two types, choose the more specific one and set needs_review: true.
5. PHASE / SCOPE: Assign phase and scope only if explicitly stated by a speaker. If not mentioned, use null.
6. CONFIDENCE PROPAGATION: If any source utterance has confidence < 0.85, set needs_review: true on that entity.
7. SPEAKER ATTRIBUTION: Always attribute entities to the speaker_id who stated or agreed to them.
8. CLEAN STATEMENTS: Write entity statements in clean, professional, active-voice prose. Do not quote the speaker verbatim — paraphrase into a formal requirement/decision/action/risk statement.
9. DISCUSSION POINTS: Capture significant topics discussed even if they did not produce a formal requirement or decision — these go in discussion_points.
10. OUTPUT FORMAT: Return ONLY the JSON object. No markdown fences, no explanation text, no preamble. Pure JSON that can be parsed directly by json.loads().

meeting_context.title: __EXTRACT: concise descriptive title of the meeting from opening utterances or dominant topic__
meeting_context.date: __EXTRACT: meeting date if mentioned (ISO 8601: YYYY-MM-DD), else null__
meeting_context.time_start: __DERIVE: utterance 0 start time formatted as HH:MM:SS__
meeting_context.time_end: __DERIVE: last utterance end time formatted as HH:MM:SS__
meeting_context.duration_seconds: __DERIVE: integer — last utterance end minus utterance 0 start__
meeting_context.meeting_type: __CLASSIFY: requirements_discovery | status_update | design_review | sprint_planning | retrospective | stakeholder_review | incident_postmortem | sales_discovery | strategy_planning | onboarding | vendor_evaluation | other__

project.name: __EXTRACT: project or product name, null if not mentioned__
project.client: __EXTRACT: client or customer organisation, null if internal meeting__
project.domain: __EXTRACT: business domain e.g. EdTech, FinTech, Healthcare, Internal IT, null if unclear__

meeting_context.language: __DETECT: ISO 639-1 language code of the transcript e.g. en, fr, de__

participants.speaker_id: __FROM_TRANSCRIPT: speaker_id exactly as it appears e.g. SPEAKER_0__
participants.name: __EXTRACT: real name if mentioned in transcript, else use role label or speaker_id__
participants.role: __EXTRACT: job title or role if mentioned, else infer from context, else null__
participants.organisation: __EXTRACT: company or team name if mentioned, else null__
participants.utterance_count: __DERIVE: integer count of utterances for this speaker__
participants.total_speaking_seconds: __DERIVE: float — sum of (end - start) across all utterances for this speaker__
participants.role_in_meeting: __CLASSIFY: facilitator | requirements_owner | technical_lead | decision_maker | stakeholder | subject_matter_expert | note_taker | observer | unknown__

transcript_stats.total_utterances: __DERIVE: integer count of all utterance objects__
transcript_stats.avg_confidence: __DERIVE: float — mean of all confidence values, rounded to 3 decimal places__
transcript_stats.min_confidence: __DERIVE: float — lowest single confidence score in transcript__
transcript_stats.max_confidence: __DERIVE: float — highest single confidence score in transcript__
transcript_stats.low_confidence_utterances: __DERIVE: list of utterance_id integers where confidence < 0.85, empty list if none__
transcript_stats.coverage_gaps_seconds: __DERIVE: list of floats representing silence gaps > 2 seconds between consecutive utterances, empty list if none__

entities._type_hint: REQUIREMENT — functional capability the system or team must deliver
entities.subtype: __CLASSIFY: functional | non_functional | security | compliance | constraint | business_rule | integration | data | ux | performance | availability | scalability__
entities.statement: __EXTRACT: single atomic requirement in active voice, professional tone, max 2 sentences__
entities.detail: __EXTRACT: any elaboration, exception, or clarification the speaker added — null if none__
entities.acceptance_criteria: __EXTRACT: any measurable target mentioned e.g. < 3 seconds, 99.9% uptime — null if not stated__
entities.phase: __EXTRACT: phase label if explicitly stated e.g. phase_1, phase_2 — null if not mentioned__
entities.module: __EXTRACT: system module or component e.g. student, payment, auth, api — null if not mentioned__
entities.priority: __INFER: high | medium | low — based on speaker language: must/critical=high, should=medium, nice-to-have=low__
source_utterance_ids: __LIST: integer utterance_ids that contain evidence for this requirement__
entities.confidence: __DERIVE: float — avg confidence of source utterances__
entities.needs_review: __DERIVE: true if any source confidence < 0.85 OR statement is ambiguous, else false__
tags: __GENERATE: 2-6 lowercase snake_case tags relevant to this requirement__

entities._type_hint: DECISION — an agreement, choice, or resolution made during the meeting
entities.subtype: __CLASSIFY: scope | technology | process | design | commercial | resourcing | timeline | policy | other__
entities.statement: __EXTRACT: clear one-sentence statement of what was decided, in past tense__
entities.detail: __EXTRACT: rationale, context, or conditions attached to this decision — null if none__
entities.alternatives_considered: __EXTRACT: any alternative options that were mentioned and rejected — null if none__
entities.phase: __EXTRACT: phase this decision applies to — null if not mentioned__
entities.module: __EXTRACT: module or area affected — null if system-wide or not mentioned__
entities.attributed_to: __FROM_TRANSCRIPT: speaker_id who made or proposed the decision__
agreed_by: __LIST: speaker_ids of all participants who explicitly agreed__
entities.confidence: __DERIVE: float — avg confidence of source utterances__

entities._type_hint: ACTION ITEM — a concrete task assigned to or implied for a specific owner
entities.subtype: __CLASSIFY: documentation | development | research | scheduling | review | communication | procurement | testing | design | legal | other__
entities.statement: __EXTRACT: imperative task statement starting with a verb e.g. 'Schedule Phase 2 requirements session'__
entities.detail: __EXTRACT: any additional context, constraints, or success criteria — null if none__
entities.owner: __EXTRACT: speaker_id or role name of the person responsible — 'unassigned' if not clear__
entities.due: __EXTRACT: deadline or timeframe if mentioned e.g. 'next sprint', '2026-03-20' — 'tbd' if not mentioned__
entities.phase: __EXTRACT: phase this action belongs to — null if not applicable__
source_utterance_ids: __LIST: integer utterance_ids, use empty list [] if action was implied not explicit__
entities.confidence: __DERIVE: float or null if action was implied__

entities._type_hint: RISK — a threat, uncertainty, or concern that could negatively impact the project
entities.subtype: __CLASSIFY: compliance | scope | technical | resource | timeline | commercial | security | operational | external | other__
entities.statement: __EXTRACT: clear risk statement in the form 'Risk that [X] may result in [Y]'__
entities.detail: __EXTRACT: elaboration on impact, trigger, or suggested mitigation — null if none__
entities.impact: __INFER: high | medium | low — based on consequences described or implied__
entities.likelihood: __INFER: high | medium | low — based on language and context__
entities.mitigation: __EXTRACT: any mitigation strategy mentioned by speakers — null if none discussed__
entities.phase: __EXTRACT: phase most affected — null if not phase-specific__
source_utterance_ids: __LIST: integer utterance_ids, use empty list [] if risk was derived not explicit__
entities.attributed_to: __FROM_TRANSCRIPT: speaker_id who raised it, or 'derived' if inferred by analyst__

entities._type_hint: QUESTION — an open question raised that was not fully answered in the meeting
entities.subtype: __CLASSIFY: technical | commercial | scope | process | legal | resource | other__
entities.statement: __EXTRACT: the open question, phrased as a question__
entities.context: __EXTRACT: why this question matters or what decision it blocks — null if not clear__
entities.assigned_to: __EXTRACT: speaker_id who should answer it — 'unassigned' if not clear__
entities.phase: __EXTRACT: phase this question relates to — null if not phase-specific__

entities._type_hint: BLOCKER — something actively preventing progress right now
entities.subtype: __CLASSIFY: technical | resource | dependency | approval | information | environment | other__
entities.statement: __EXTRACT: what is blocked and what is blocking it__
entities.detail: __EXTRACT: impact on timeline or deliverables — null if not mentioned__
entities.owner: __EXTRACT: speaker_id or role responsible for resolving — 'unassigned' if not clear__
entities.blocking_since: __EXTRACT: duration or date if mentioned — null if not mentioned__
entities.phase: __EXTRACT: phase affected — null if not mentioned__

entities._type_hint: DISCUSSION POINT — a significant topic discussed that did not produce a formal entity but is worth recording
entities.subtype: __CLASSIFY: context | background | clarification | concern | suggestion | feedback | update | other__
entities.statement: __EXTRACT: one-sentence summary of what was discussed__
entities.detail: __EXTRACT: key points or perspectives raised — null if sufficiently covered in statement__
entities.phase: __EXTRACT: phase this relates to — null if general__

roadmap._note: Only populate if phases were explicitly discussed. Omit entirely if no phasing was mentioned.
roadmap.label: __EXTRACT: human-readable label e.g. Phase 1__
roadmap.module: __EXTRACT: primary module or focus area for this phase__
roadmap.status: __CLASSIFY: active | backlog | in_progress | complete | on_hold | undefined__
roadmap.description: __EXTRACT: brief description of what this phase delivers — null if not mentioned__
entity_ids: __LIST: all entity_ids (REQ, DEC, ACT, RISK etc.) scoped to this phase__

open_items._note: Consolidates all unresolved questions, risks needing review, and blockers into one tracking list.
open_items.entity_id: __REFERENCE: entity_id of the open item e.g. QST-001, RISK-002, BLK-001__
open_items.owner: __REFERENCE: owner or assigned_to from the entity__
open_items.due: __REFERENCE: due date if applicable, else tbd__

requirements_by_subtype.other: __DERIVE: count of all remaining requirement subtypes__

by_priority.high: __DERIVE: count of entities with priority == high__
by_priority.medium: __DERIVE: count of entities with priority == medium__
by_priority.low: __DERIVE: count of entities with priority == low__

unassigned_action_items: __LIST: entity_ids of action_items where owner == unassigned__

phase_breakdown.__EXTRACT: phase_1__: __DERIVE: count of entities in this phase__
phase_breakdown.__EXTRACT: phase_N__: __DERIVE: count of entities in this phase__
phase_breakdown.all_phases: __DERIVE: count of entities scoped to all_phases__
phase_breakdown.unscoped: __DERIVE: count of entities with phase == null__

meeting_health.action_items_assigned: __DERIVE: integer — count of action_items where owner != unassigned__
meeting_health.low_confidence_entity_count: __DERIVE: integer — count of entities with needs_review == true__
